name: Korean Peninsula
description: The peninsula between the Yellow Sea and the Sea of Japan, core territory of the Koreanic peoples.

periods.note: Fragmentation into Goguryeo, Baekje, and Silla; deep cultural and linguistic unity despite political division.
First unification of the peninsula; Golden Age of Buddhist culture.
Resistance against Mongol (Yuan) invasions; source of the name 'Korea'.
The longest-running Confucian state; high administrative continuity.
Colonial rule; ethnic Korean majority under Japanese imperial administration.
